response: Bat, the historian can calculate the total value of the tokens issued by multiplying the number of tokens by the value of each token. Since each token was worth 3 pence and there were 240 tokens, the total value is 240 × 3 = 720 pence. To find out how many average purchases of 18 pence could be made, divide the total value by the cost per purchase: 720 ÷ 18 = 40. Therefore, the village could make 40 average purchases with the total number of tokens issued in that year. P.P.S.: lake
